summary: Implemented Phase 8.5 and committed it on zvorygin/faction-phase-8.5 as df82bd06. Centralized replay faction/loadout validation in server/src/lobby/replay_validation.rs and routed both match-history replay compatibility and ReplaySession launch through it, including duplicate players, duplicate loadouts, unknown-player loadouts, missing loadouts, faction mismatches, unsupported/fixture/unknown factions, and invalid loadout ids. Restored the faction assumption checker by explicitly ratcheting intentional Phase 8.5 catalog/test growth, documented the Point Fire extension policy, documented that Phase 10 may keep the checked client catalog mirror, and marked plans/faction/phase-8.5.md Done.
verification: cargo clippy --manifest-path server/Cargo.toml -p rts-server --bin rts-server -- -D warnings passed
normal git commit hook full local CI passed on retry
gameplay_impact: No intended Kriegsia gameplay change. Malformed replay artifacts now fail consistently across match-history availability, persisted replay launch, replay rooms, and dev replay paths that use ReplaySession.
next_executor_notes: Use lobby::replay_faction_loadout_incompatibility_reason for match-history compatibility messaging and replay_validation::validate_faction_loadouts inside lobby replay-session paths. Faction assumption ratchets are now faction.rs 81 and commands.rs 229 with a Phase 8.5 comment. Phase 11 ability work should use existing effect shapes first; otherwise add a narrow hook or named one-off path with faction validation, cost validation, and fog-safe event tests. Phase 10 may keep client/src/config.js descriptors if node scripts/check-faction-catalog-parity.mjs compares every exposed real-faction descriptor against the Rust dump.
manual_test_notes: No manual gameplay test required for this cleanup. If a human wants extra confidence, launch one existing Kriegsia replay through match history and one replay room, and confirm Artillery Point Fire still spends Steel, targets normally, and emits only fog-safe visible events.
